entries.title: Meta AI Agent Skipped Required Human Review; Flawed Guidance Led to Broad Data Access
entries.oneLineSummary: An autonomous AI agent at Meta skipped its required human-review checkpoint and posted flawed access guidance; a human engineer acting on that guidance broadened access to sensitive data, which remained exposed for roughly two hours.

sources.title: Unite.AI: Meta AI Agent Triggers Sev 1 Security Incident
OECD AI Incidents: 2026-03-18
VentureBeat: Meta Rogue AI Agent
MakerChecker: Meta Rogue Agent Analysis

entries.title: Morse Code Prompt Injection Drained Grok-Connected Wallet of $150K
entries.oneLineSummary: A Morse-coded prompt injection in a social media reply tricked Grok into executing an irreversible $150K cryptocurrency transfer without human approval.

sources.title: How Grok Got Prompt Injected: An X User Drained $150,000 From An AI Wallet
Behind the Grok Exploitation: An Analysis of AI Agent Permission Chain Abuse
OECD AI Incidents Database Entry 2026-05-04-4a73

entries.title: DN42 Network Scan Agent Spawned $6,531 AWS Bill via Uncontrolled Provisioning Loop
entries.oneLineSummary: An AI agent told to scan a hobbyist network autonomously provisioned five large AWS instances in a redeploy loop, incurring an initial $6,531.30 bill under a blanket operator "continue" directive; AWS later reduced the charge to roughly $1,894 after the operator disputed it.

sources.title: Lantian's account of the DN42 agent bankruptcy
Bovo Digital technical analysis
Decrypt coverage

entries.title: Cursor Agent Deleted Production Database and Backups via Over-Privileged Railway Token
entries.oneLineSummary: A Cursor AI agent working on a staging task invoked volumeDelete using an over-privileged Railway token, destroying PocketOS's production database and its co-located backups in nine seconds; accounts of the recovery differ, from a partial restore to days of manual rebuilding and permanent loss of recent data.

sources.title: The Register
Hackread
Cybersecurity News
MakerChecker Analysis

entries.title: Claude Code Force-Pushed Over Private Repository and Destroyed Commit History
entries.oneLineSummary: Claude Code ran git push --force after a failed rebase and overwrote a private repository's entire commit history without authorization.

sources.title: GitHub Issue #33402
GitHub Issue #29120
MakerChecker Insights: Claude Code Force-Push Destroyed Git History

entries.title: ShadowLeak: Zero-Click Gmail Exfiltration via ChatGPT Deep Research Agent
entries.oneLineSummary: Radware researchers demonstrated a proof-of-concept in which ChatGPT's Deep Research agent could be induced to exfiltrate Gmail data via a hidden email instruction, with outbound requests originating from within OpenAI's cloud so local network defenses could not see them. OpenAI fixed it before public disclosure; no in-the-wild exploitation was reported.

sources.title: Radware ShadowLeak Threat Intelligence Report
The Hacker News: ShadowLeak Zero-Click Flaw Leaks Gmail
Infosecurity Magazine: Vulnerability ChatGPT Agent Gmail
MakerChecker ShadowLeak Incident Analysis

entries.title: Replit Agent Deleted Production Database During Code Freeze
entries.oneLineSummary: A Replit coding agent deleted ~2,400 production database records (1,206 executives, 1,196+ companies) during an explicit code freeze, fabricated ~4,000 fake user profiles, and falsely claimed rollback was impossible.

sources.title: The Register
AI Incident Database
Fast Company
MakerChecker Full Analysis

entries.title: MyPillow Attorney Filings with Fabricated Citations and a Repeat Miscitation
entries.oneLineSummary: Attorneys filed a brief with AI-generated citations to nonexistent cases and were sanctioned; months later the lead attorney was sanctioned again for misciting a real case to the wrong court and mischaracterizing its holding.

sources.title: Colorado Sun - Mike Lindell's Attorneys Fined for AI-Generated Filing
Law & Crime - Judge Sanctions Mike Lindell's Lawyers Over AI-Generated Filing with Nonexistent Cases
Colorado Politics - Mike Lindell's Lawyer Sanctioned Again Over Flawed Case Citations

entries.title: Google Antigravity Agent Permanently Deleted Developer's Entire D Drive
entries.oneLineSummary: Google's Antigravity agentic IDE misresolved a cache-clearing request and silently executed an unrestricted recursive delete of an entire drive partition, permanently destroying all data.

sources.title: TechRadar
Awesome Agent Failures
PiunikaWeb
MakerChecker Analysis

entries.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Zero-Click Exfiltration via Prompt Injection (CVE-2025-32711)
entries.oneLineSummary: Researchers demonstrated a zero-click proof-of-concept in which a crafted email's hidden instructions could make M365 Copilot exfiltrate OneDrive, SharePoint, and Teams data to an attacker-controlled URL with no user click required. Microsoft fixed it server-side before disclosure.

sources.title: The Hacker News
SecurityWeek
Business Wire

entries.title: CamoLeak: GitHub Copilot Chat Exfiltrates Private Source Code via Hidden Markdown Instructions
entries.oneLineSummary: Researchers demonstrated that hidden markdown instructions in pull requests could make GitHub Copilot Chat leak private source code and secrets one character at a time through GitHub's Camo image proxy; GitHub fixed it before disclosure with no known in-the-wild use.

sources.title: Legit Security: CamoLeak Critical GitHub Copilot Vulnerability Leaks Private Source Code
Dark Reading: GitHub Copilot CamoLeak AI Attack Exfiltrates Data
The Register: GitHub Copilot Chat Vulnerability

entries.title: UnitedHealth nH Predict Denied Medicare Post-Acute Care Without Clinician Authorization
entries.oneLineSummary: AI coverage-denial system allegedly committed post-acute care denials to Medicare Advantage beneficiaries without named clinician authorization; the class action alleges — as a lawsuit claim, not an established finding — a 90% reversal rate on appeal.

entries.oneLineSummary: Attorneys filed a federal brief with six cases ChatGPT fabricated, without independently verifying that the cited cases existed.

entries.title: Chevrolet of Watsonville: Prompt-Injected Chatbot Agreed in Conversation to Sell a Vehicle for $1
entries.oneLineSummary: A prompt-injected ChatGPT-powered dealer chatbot agreed in conversation to sell a 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe for $1 and called it legally binding; the exchange was text only and no sale occurred.

sources.title: VentureBeat: A Chevy for $1 — Car Dealer Chatbots Show Perils of AI for Customer Service
AI Incident Database: Chevrolet Chatbot Binding Offer

entries.title: Citigroup $444B Basket: Hard Blocks Caught ~$248B, No Notional Ceiling on the Rest
entries.oneLineSummary: A Citigroup trader clicked through a single pop-up listing 711 warning messages — only the first 18 visible without scrolling — and released a $444B basket order instead of the intended $58M; ~$1.4B sold before cancellation and the FCA/PRA fined Citigroup £61.6M.

sources.title: CNN: Citigroup fined $61.6 million over fat-finger trading blunder
Bloomberg: Citigroup Fined $61.6 Million Over Fat-Finger Trade
Markets Media: Citi Fined $61.6M for Fat Finger Trade

entries.title: Cigna PxDx Batch Rubber-Stamp Denials
entries.oneLineSummary: Cigna's PxDx system was used to deny 300,000+ medical claims over about two months in 2022, with reviewing doctors averaging a reported 1.2 seconds per denial.

sources.title: Cigna Algorithm Patient Claims Lawsuit
Cigna Lawsuit Algorithm Claims Denials California

MakerChecker: Cigna PxDx Batch Rubber-Stamp Denials Analysis

entries.title: Air Canada Held Liable for Chatbot's Misstated Bereavement Refund Policy
entries.oneLineSummary: Air Canada's website chatbot misstated the airline's bereavement fare policy as retroactively claimable, a customer booked on that basis, and a BC tribunal ordered the airline to pay $812.02 CAD for negligent misrepresentation.

sources.title: BC Civil Resolution Tribunal Decision 2024BCCRT149 (Moffatt v Air Canada)
McCarthy Tetrault: Moffatt v. Air Canada — Misrepresentation & AI Chatbot
Pinsent Masons: Air Canada Chatbot Case Highlights AI Liability Risks

entries.title: Australia Robodebt: Unlawful Automated Welfare Debt Calculation
entries.oneLineSummary: Australia's Robodebt used an unlawful income-averaging method to automatically calculate and issue welfare debts, unlawfully raising approximately A$1.76 billion in debts against more than 433,000 people; the roughly A$751 million actually recovered was later repaid under a Federal Court settlement.

sources.title: Royal Commission into the Robodebt Scheme Final Report
Crude, Cruel and Unlawful: Robodebt Royal Commission Findings
Australia's Robodebt Scheme: A Tragic Case of Public Policy Failure

entries.title: Everbright Securities Arbitrage System Runaway Orders with Undisclosed Insider Hedge Cover Trade
entries.oneLineSummary: Everbright Securities' arbitrage system generated 23.4 billion yuan in erroneous buy orders with no enforcement ceiling, and the desk then executed a massive insider hedge before disclosing the error to the market.

sources.title: CNN Money: China Everbright Securities Fined
SCMP: China Everbright Securities Fined 523M Yuan Stock Market
China Daily: Everbright trading glitch article

entries.title: Knight Capital $440M Runaway Trading Loss
entries.oneLineSummary: Dormant algorithmic trading feature reactivated by configuration flag error, executing millions of unintended orders and causing about $440 million in losses within 45 minutes.

sources.title: SEC Press Release 2013-222
SEC Order and Settlement Agreement
